headline: The turbulent life of Pavel Talankin, the Oscar-winning director in the Russian government's crosshairs
description: The global release of 'Mr. Nobody Against Putin,' which exposes Russian war propaganda to primary school students in the Urals, brought the film's director, Pavel Talankin, to public attention. But the Kremlin labeled him a 'foreign agent,' increasing the threats against the filmmaker, now living in exile in Prague.

In the United Kingdom, Prime Minister Keir Starmer once again weakened by the Mandelson affair
description: According to revelations by the Guardian on Thursday, the former ambassador to the United States, implicated in the Jeffrey Epstein affair, was sent to Washington without having received the necessary security clearance. Keir Starmer said on Friday that he was stunned not to have been informed.

headline: France's 'Clair Obscur' wins BAFTA for best video game
description: Having sold more than five million copies, the first game from Sandfall Interactive, 'Clair Obscur: Expedition 33,' has become a global phenomenon since its release last year.

headline: The radical eye and many lives of photographer Lee Miller at the Musée d'art moderne de Paris
description: A retrospective rich with vintage prints traces the career of this American force who constantly reinvented herself, from surrealist artist to war correspondent.

headline: 10 family outings in and around Paris this spring
description: Sing a duet, wander through the Musée d'Orsay, marvel at the Musée du Quai Branly, then hop on a scooter from one covered passageway to another… There is no shortage of fun activities in the Paris region to keep kids and teenagers entertained.

description: Despite major pressure on the power grid and the risk of rising greenhouse gas emissions, Dublin wants to keep building these facilities for tech giants and establish itself as a European leader in the sector.

headline: The pitfalls of Hungary's post-Orban transition
description: Riding a powerful wave of opposition to the outgoing government, Hungary's new prime minister, Péter Magyar, now has to balance the expectations of both left- and right-wing voters while clarifying his positions, especially regarding Ukraine.

headline: How Algeria tried to erase the two suicide attacks during Pope Leo's visit
description: Two men blew themselves up on Monday about 40 kilometers from Algiers, without causing any casualties other than themselves. Authorities sought to impose silence on the failed terrorist attack from the official narrative of the historic visit.
